body: ## Purpose Reverting to a constant canopy height. Why? - It didnt have much of an impact on accuracy and if anything seems changing back to a constant improves bias - When we calibrated our current parameterization, the coefficient for multiplying h_canopy for displacement height and z_0b became very small, indicating a lack of dependence on canopy height. Agreed that these should have some spatial dependence, but I think we should rethink it. Not that we need to try and match CLM, but: - CLM models canopy height as a constant of 0.5m (which was very similar to what we were originally doing) - CLM models displacement height as 0.67 x canopy_height x function of LAI/SAI. The function of LAI/SAI is between zero and 1. Therefore displacement height is <~0.335 m always. Our displacement height can become very large, so that we need to clip it if d > z_atmos - z_sfc. This is clearly not physical. - Similar for roughness - the upshot is a roughness length between 0.01 (bare soil) and 0.06 m in CLM. Long runs: https://buildkite.com/clima/climaland-long-runs/builds/4723 Alexis' long run on Friday: https://buildkite.com/clima/climaland-long-runs/builds/4720 ## Notes
title: revert to constant canopy height

body: some fixes to docs and tests
title: Ar/inland water slab fixes
